# ROCKYT AI SOCIAL MEDIA & AD AUTOMATION API REFERENCE
# Base URL: https://rockyt.io/api/v1
# Version: 1.0.0
# Description: Unified Social Media & Ad Operations REST API for AI Agents and Developers.

================================================================================
1. AUTHENTICATION & HEADERS
================================================================================

All API requests to Rockyt endpoints must be authenticated via Bearer token in the Authorization header.

  Header: Authorization
  Format: Bearer <YOUR_API_KEY>
  Example: Authorization: Bearer rkt_live_a1b2c3d4e5f6...

API keys can be generated directly in the Rockyt Dashboard under Developer Settings / APIs.
API keys carry full user permissions. Keep them secret. Do not expose keys in client-side code.

Standard Request Headers:
  Content-Type: application/json
  Accept: application/json

================================================================================
2. ACCOUNT LIMITS & PLAN QUOTAS
================================================================================

Rockyt enforces per-account connected social channel limits based on subscription plan:
  - Growth Plan / Trial / Free: Maximum 1 Connected Account
  - Scale Plan: Maximum 10 Connected Accounts

Connected Account Quota Endpoint:
  GET /api/v1/me/dashboard-usage
  Response:
  {
    "connectedAccounts": 1,
    "maxAccounts": 1
  }

================================================================================
3. ENDPOINT REFERENCE
================================================================================

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3.1 GET /api/v1/keys
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Description: List active API keys for the authenticated user.

Response (200 OK):
[
  {
    "id": "key_uuid_12345",
    "key_prefix": "rkt_live",
    "created_at": "2025-01-15T12:00:00.000Z"
  }
]

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3.2 POST /api/v1/keys
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Description: Issue a new API key for the authenticated user.

Response (201 Created):
{
  "key": "rkt_live_7f8a9b0c1d2e3f4a5b6c7d8e9f0a1b2c"
}

Note: The full key is returned only once at creation time.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3.3 DELETE /api/v1/keys/:id
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Description: Revoke an existing API key.

Path Parameter:
  id (string, required): The ID of the key to revoke.

Response (200 OK):
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "API key revoked successfully"
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3.4 POST /api/v1/posts
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Description: Create and publish or schedule a social media post across connected channels.

Request Body (JSON):
{
  "content": "Shipped v2.0! Check out our new AI features.",
  "platforms": ["twitter", "linkedin", "instagram", "facebook"],
  "scheduled_for": "2025-01-15T09:00:00Z",
  "media_urls": ["https://example.com/demo.png"]
}

Field Descriptions:
  - content (string, required): Text content of the post. Max length varies by platform.
  - platforms (array of strings, required): Supported platform IDs:
      ["facebook", "instagram", "tiktok", "twitter", "linkedin", "youtube", "threads", "reddit", "pinterest", "bluesky"]
  - scheduled_for (string, optional): ISO-8601 timestamp for scheduling. Omit for instant publication.
  - media_urls (array of strings, optional): Publicly accessible URLs for image or video attachments.

Response (200 OK):
{
  "id": "post_99887766",
  "status": "scheduled",
  "platforms": ["twitter", "linkedin", "instagram", "facebook"],
  "scheduled_for": "2025-01-15T09:00:00Z",
  "created_at": "2025-01-14T18:30:00.000Z"
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3.5 GET /api/v1/posts
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Description: Retrieve recent posts and publication status.

Query Parameters:
  - limit (integer, optional): Number of records to return (default: 20, max: 100).
  - page (integer, optional): Page number (default: 1).

Response (200 OK):
{
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "post_99887766",
      "content": "Shipped v2.0! Check out our new AI features.",
      "status": "published",
      "platforms": ["twitter", "linkedin"],
      "published_at": "2025-01-15T09:00:00Z"
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "total": 1,
    "page": 1,
    "limit": 20
  }
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
3.6 POST /api/v1/connect/:platform
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Description: Initiate OAuth connection flow for a social platform.

Path Parameter:
  platform (string, required): Target platform ID (e.g. "facebook", "instagram", "tiktok", "twitter", "linkedin").

Response (200 OK):
{
  "auth_url": "https://rockyt.io/connect/oauth/authorize?platform=instagram&session_id=..."
}

================================================================================
4. SUPPORTED PLATFORMS
================================================================================

Rockyt unifies operations across 15+ social & advertising platforms:
  1. Meta Ads & Facebook Pages ("facebook")
  2. Instagram Business & Creators ("instagram")
  3. TikTok for Business ("tiktok")
  4. Twitter / X Ads & API v2 ("twitter")
  5. LinkedIn Pages & Profiles ("linkedin")
  6. YouTube Shorts & Channel API ("youtube")
  7. Meta Threads ("threads")
  8. Reddit Ads & Community API ("reddit")
  9. Pinterest Business ("pinterest")
 10. Bluesky Social AT Protocol ("bluesky")

================================================================================
5. CODE EXAMPLES
================================================================================

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5.1 cURL
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
curl -X POST "https://rockyt.io/api/v1/posts" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer rkt_live_YOUR_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "content": "Shipped v2.0 via Rockyt API!",
    "platforms": ["twitter", "linkedin", "instagram"]
  }'

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5.2 Node.js (JavaScript / TypeScript)
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
import Rockyt from '@rockyt/node';

const rockyt = new Rockyt('rkt_live_YOUR_KEY');

const post = await rockyt.posts.create({
  content: 'Shipped v2.0 via Rockyt API!',
  platforms: ['twitter', 'linkedin', 'instagram']
});

console.log('Published post ID:', post.id);

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5.3 Python
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
import requests

headers = {
    "Authorization": "Bearer rkt_live_YOUR_KEY",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

payload = {
    "content": "Shipped v2.0 via Rockyt API!",
    "platforms": ["twitter", "linkedin", "instagram"]
}

response = requests.post("https://rockyt.io/api/v1/posts", headers=headers, json=payload)
print(response.json())

================================================================================
6. ERROR CODES & HANDLING
================================================================================

Rockyt returns standard HTTP status codes:
  - 200 OK: Request succeeded.
  - 201 Created: Resource created.
  - 400 Bad Request: Invalid payload or missing parameters.
  - 401 Unauthorized: Invalid or missing API key.
  - 403 Forbidden: Account limit reached or subscription inactive.
  - 429 Too Many Requests: Rate limit exceeded (100 req/min per key).
  - 500 Internal Server Error: Server side exception.

Error Response Format:
{
  "error": "Error description message",
  "code": "ACCOUNT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
}

================================================================================
END OF DOCUMENTATION
================================================================================
